qa_pairs.question: What actions does the Alliance support team take when they detect a compromised virtual machine (VM)?
qa_pairs.answer: Our support team confirms the compromise by investigating network traffic logs and other sources. Following confirmation, the VM is shut down and locked at the sysadmin level, and you are notified by email.

qa_pairs.question: Why is it necessary to build a new virtual machine (VM) after an existing one has been compromised?
qa_pairs.answer: It is necessary to build a new VM because you cannot start an administratively locked VM. Additionally, while it is relatively safe to extract data, the contents of the compromised VM are no longer trustworthy, compelling you to build a new one.

qa_pairs.question: What is the first step a user should take when initiating a data recovery plan for a compromised virtual machine (VM)?
qa_pairs.answer: The first step is to send an email to cloud@tech.alliancecan.ca outlining your recovery plan. If access to the filesystem is required for recovery, the cloud support team will then unlock the volume.
